title.en: DAIRY PRODUCTS, e.g. MILK, BUTTER OR CHEESE; MILK OR CHEESE SUBSTITUTES; PREPARATION THEREOF (preservation thereof A23B11/00)
Concentration, evaporation or drying (products obtained thereby A23C9/00)
Drying in thin layers
by spraying into a gas stream
Concentration by freezing out the water
Foam drying (A23C1/04, A23C1/08 take precedence)
Concentration by evaporation
combined with other treatment
Other dairy technology (preservation of milk or dairy products A23B11/00)
Chemical cleaning of dairy apparatus; Use of sterilisation methods therefor
Removing unwanted substances {other than lactose or milk proteins} from milk
Milk preparations; Milk powder or milk powder preparations (mixtures of whey with milk products or milk components A23C21/06; preservation of milk or milk preparations A23B11/10)
{Condensed milk; Sugared condensed milk (A23C1/06 and A23C1/12 take precedence)}
Fermented milk preparations; Treatment using microorganisms or enzymes
in which the chemical composition of the milk is modified by non-chemical treatment
Reconstituted or recombined milk products containing neither non-milk fat nor non-milk proteins
containing additives (fermented milk preparations containing additives A23C9/13)
Agglomerating or granulating milk powder; Making instant milk powder; Products obtained thereby (A23C9/18 takes precedence)
Milk in dried and compressed or semi-solid form
Dietetic milk products not covered by groups A23C9/12 - A23C9/18
Milk substitutes, e.g. coffee whitener compositions (cheese substitutes A23C20/00; preservation of milk substitutes A23B11/20; butter substitutes A23D)

containing at least one non-milk component as source of fats or proteins (addition of non-milk fats or non-milk proteins in making cheese curd A23C19/055; whey or whey preparations containing non-milk components as source of fats or proteins A23C21/04)
Cream; Cream preparations (preservation of cream or cream preparations A23B11/30; ice-cream A23G9/00); Making thereof
Cream preparations
Butter; Butter preparations; Making thereof (preservation of butter or butter preparations A23B11/40; butter substitutes A23D)
Making thereof
Butter preparations

Buttermilk; Buttermilk preparations (milk preparations, milk powder or milk powder preparations in which the chemical composition of the milk is modified by non-chemical treatment A23C9/14; preservation of buttermilk or buttermilk preparations A23B11/50)
containing, or treated with, microorganisms or enzymes
Cheese; Cheese preparations; Making thereof (cheese substitutes A23C20/00; preservation of cheese or cheese preparations A23B11/60)
Making cheese curd
Treating cheese curd after whey separation; Products obtained thereby
Treating cheese after having reached its definite form, e.g. ripening or smoking
Cheese substitutes (A23C19/055, A23C19/093 take precedence; preservation of cheese substitutes A23B11/70)
{mainly containing proteins from pulses or oilseeds}
containing neither milk components, nor caseinate, nor lactose, as sources of fats, proteins or carbohydrates

Whey; Whey preparations (concentration, evaporation or drying A23C1/00; milk preparations, milk powder or milk powder preparations in which the chemical composition of the milk is modified by non-chemical treatment A23C9/14; preservation of whey or whey preparations A23B11/80)
containing, or treated with, microorganisms or enzymes
containing non-milk components as source of fats or proteins
Mixtures of whey with milk products or milk components
containing other organic additives, e.g. vegetable or animal products
containing inorganic additives
Other dairy products
Special features
Physical treatment of dairy products
Conventional drying followed by fluidised bed drying; Fluidised bed drying at the bottom of a spray-drier
General processes or apparatus not classified in A23B11/1375 for direct contact of a falling film of liquid with steam
High pressure treatment
Treatment using membranes, including sterile filtration
Separating and blending
Whipping, foaming, frothing or aerating dairy products
Microencapsulation; Encapsulation of particles
Biochemical treatment
Enzymatic treatment
Treatment with microorganisms
Aspects relating to animal feed or genotype
Milk or milk products from transgenic animals
Animal milk with modified composition due to a specific feed
Animal milk with modified composition due to manipulation of the animal, e.g. animal milk comprising antibodies, selection of animals having specific genotypes
Use or particular additives or ingredients
Milk products enriched with milk fat globule membrane
Dairy products containing sterols or sterol derivatives
Use of plant extracts, including purified and isolated derivatives thereof, as ingredient in dairy products
Inert gas treatment, using, e.g. noble gases or CO2, including CO2 liberated by chemical reaction; Carbonation of milk products
Particular aspects related to cheese
Emulsifying cheese
Cheese characterised by a specific form
Shredded non-dried cheese
Compacting shredded or ground cheese other than curd or small cheese pieces without melting
Cheese with fat content lower than 0.5%, including cheese from skim milk, i.e. no addition of fats
Blue veined cheeses, e.g. Roquefort, Gorgonzola, Stilton
Smoking of cheese or curd; Smoked cheeses; Smoking of milk components before conversion to cheese
Breaded cheese products, e.g. coating with a batter mix or other adhesive material followed by breading with crumbs or other particulate coating
Cheese from buttermilk
Particular aspects or types of dairy products
Concentrated yoghurt products, e.g. labneh, yoghurt cheese, non-dried non-frozen solid or semi-solid yoghurt products other than spreads; Strained yoghurt; Removal of whey from yoghurt
Spreadable dairy products
Frozen dairy products
Dry foaming beverage creamer or whitener, e.g. gas injected or containing carbonation or foaming agents, for causing foaming when reconstituted
Dairy compositions providing for tooth protective effects, e.g. anticariogenic effects
Aspects relating to packaging
Gelled or liquid milk product, e.g. yoghurt, cottage cheese or pudding being one of the separate layers of a multilayered soft or liquid food product
Dairy products filled into pressurised containers with dispensing means for atomisation or foaming
Separate packaging or storing of foodstuffs or ingredients to be mixed prior to use or serving, e.g. storing into separate compartments of a single container or into separate containers
